description: Aster P0 全栈贯通 — 骨架到可玩游戏：wgpu 渲染、编辑器项目工作流、运行时游戏循环、Rapier 物理、Rhai 脚本、资产导入管线、CLI 构建运行

userStories.title: Add TimeState struct to engine-core
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TimeState struct to track delta time, fixed timestep, and frame counting for the game loop.
acceptanceCriteria: TimeState struct with fields: delta_seconds, fixed_delta_seconds (default 1.0/60.0), total_time, frame_index, time_scale (default 1.0)
TimeState::new() with sensible defaults
TimeState::update(dt) to advance frame counter and accumulate time
Unit tests for time accumulation
cargo test -p engine-core passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add InputState struct to engine-platform
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an InputState struct to track keyboard, mouse, and scroll state per frame with pressed/released/held semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: InputState struct with: keys (HashMap<KeyCode, KeyState>), mouse_buttons, cursor_position, mouse_delta, scroll_delta
KeyState enum: Pressed (first frame), Held, Released (first frame), Up
InputState::begin_frame() resets transient states (Pressed→Held, Released→Up, deltas to zero)
InputState::is_pressed(key), is_held(key), is_released(key) query methods
winit KeyCode and MouseButton re-exported or wrapped
cargo test -p engine-platform passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add ActionMap for input action binding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ActionMap to bind multiple physical keys to logical action names so game code queries actions instead of raw keys.
acceptanceCriteria: ActionMap struct stores HashMap<String, Vec<KeyCode>> bindings
ActionMap::bind(action_name, key) adds a binding
ActionMap::action_pressed(&input_state, action_name) returns true if any bound key is Pressed
ActionMap::action_held(&input_state, action_name) returns true if any bound key is Held or Pressed
Default bindings: MoveForward(W/Up), MoveBack(S/Down), MoveLeft(A/Left), MoveRight(D/Right), Jump(Space)
Unit tests for single and multi-key bindings
cargo test -p engine-platform passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add ProjectContext struct to engine-editor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ProjectContext struct that bundles ProjectManifest, Scene, AssetDatabase, and project root path into one handle for the editor shell.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectContext struct with fields: manifest (ProjectManifest), scene (Scene), asset_db (AssetDatabase), project_root (PathBuf)
ProjectContext::open(project_root) -> EngineResult<Self>: loads aster.project.toml, parses default_scene JSON, scans asset_root
Loading errors produce EngineError with descriptive message, not panic
Unit test: opening examples/project returns Ok
cargo test -p engine-editor passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Create WgpuRenderDevice struct with instance, adapter, device init
userStories.description: As a developer, I need engine-render-wgpu to provide a real WgpuRenderDevice that initializes wgpu instance, adapter, and device.
acceptanceCriteria: WgpuRenderDevice struct in engine-render-wgpu with fields: instance, adapter, device, queue
WgpuRenderDevice::new(window: &Window) -> EngineResult<Self> using pollster::block_on or async init
Implements engine_render::RenderDevice trait
Requests adapter with power preference HighPerformance
Device descriptor requests default limits + TextureFormat::Bgra8UnormSrgb for swapchain
Fallback to any adapter if HighPerformance not available
cargo build -p engine-render-wgpu passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add surface and swapchain to WgpuRenderDevice
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WgpuRenderDevice to create a wgpu Surface from a window handle and configure a swapchain for presenting rendered frames.
acceptanceCriteria: WgpuRenderDevice gains surface: wgpu::Surface and config: wgpu::SurfaceConfiguration
Surface created from winit Window via create_surface()
Swapchain configured with present_mode AutoVsync or Fifo, format Bgra8UnormSrgb
configure() method sets up surface with adapter info and window dimensions
Integration test verifies surface creation succeeds with a real winit window
cargo test -p engine-render-wgpu passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement swapchain resize and recreation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WgpuRenderDevice to handle window resize by recreating the swapchain without leaking resources.
acceptanceCriteria: WgpuRenderDevice::resize(width, height) reconfigures surface and recreates swapchain
Old swapchain resources properly dropped before recreation
Resize to zero area handled: suspend rendering until valid dimensions
Called from winit WindowEvent::Resized in the runtime/editor event loop
Manual test: run window, resize, verify no crash and rendering continues
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add built-in debug meshes (cube, sphere, plane)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need built-in procedural meshes so the renderer can draw geometry without external asset files.
acceptanceCriteria: DebugMesh enum with Cube, Sphere(segments), Plane variants
Each variant generates Vec<Vertex> (position, normal, uv) and Vec<u32> indices
Vertex struct: position [f32; 3], normal [f32; 3], uv [f32; 2]
MeshBuffers struct holding vertex_buffer, index_buffer, index_count
WgpuRenderDevice::create_mesh_buffers(&self, mesh: &DebugMesh) -> MeshBuffers uploads to GPU
Unit test verifies cube has 24 vertices (hard normals) and 36 indices
cargo test -p engine-render-wgpu passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add embedded default WGSL shader
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a default WGSL shader embedded in engine-render-wgpu that supports basic PBR-style diffuse + ambient lighting.
acceptanceCriteria: Vertex shader: transforms position by model-view-projection matrix, passes normal and uv to fragment
Fragment shader: diffuse lighting from directional light + ambient term, texture sampling if material has texture
Shader source embedded as &str constant in crate (no external file dependency)
ShaderModule created once and reused across render passes
Bind group layout: camera uniform (buffer 0), model transform uniform (buffer 1), material params + texture (binding 2/3)
Vertically compiled with naga via wgpu::Device::create_shader_module (no runtime compilation errors)
cargo test -p engine-render-wgpu passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RenderWorld extraction from Scene
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RenderWorld to extract Camera, MeshRenderer, and Light components from a Scene into renderable objects.
acceptanceCriteria: RenderWorld::extract(scene: &Scene) iterates all GameObjects in the scene
Camera component → RenderCamera { projection, view, position, near, far, fov }
MeshRenderer + Transform → RenderObject { mesh_handle, material_handle, model_matrix }
Light component → RenderLight { kind: Directional|Point|Spot, color, intensity, position, direction }
Entities without MeshRenderer or with is_active=false are skipped
Unit test: Scene with 1 Camera + 2 MeshRenderers → RenderWorld contains 1 camera + 2 objects
cargo test -p engine-render passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement WgpuRenderDevice::render() pipeline execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WgpuRenderDevice::render() to execute the full frame: clear → depth pass → opaque pass → present.
acceptanceCriteria: WgpuRenderDevice::render(frame: RenderFrame) acquires swapchain texture, creates CommandEncoder
Clear pass: color to (0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 1.0), depth to 1.0
Opaque pass: for each RenderObject, bind pipeline, set vertex/index buffers, set bind groups, draw_indexed
Camera uniform buffer created and updated each frame with view-projection matrix
Model transform uniform buffer per object (or use push constants if available)
Present: queue.submit() then swapchain present()
Integration test: create WgpuRenderDevice with window, render one frame with debug cube, no error
cargo test -p engine-render-wgpu passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Create RenderService wrapper for EditorShell
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a RenderService struct in engine-editor that wraps the RenderDevice and manages offscreen render targets for editor viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: RenderService struct: holds Box<dyn RenderDevice>, manages HashMap<String, RenderTargetDesc> for named targets
RenderService::new(device) creates service with default targets for scene_view and game_view
RenderService::render_target(&self, name) -> &RenderTargetDesc to query target dimensions
RenderService::resize_target(name, width, height) updates target and recreates dependent resources
RenderService::render_to_target(name, render_world) renders a frame to the named offscreen target
cargo test -p engine-editor passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Integrate wgpu render texture into egui Scene View
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the Scene View panel to display live wgpu-rendered 3D content instead of a placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene View panel in EditorShell receives RenderService reference
On each frame, EditorShell renders scene_view target via RenderService::render_to_target("scene_view", render_world)
Rendered texture is displayed in the egui panel area via egui::Image or texture callback
Editor camera controls (right-click drag to rotate, scroll to zoom, middle-click to pan) update scene view camera
Resize of Scene View panel triggers RenderService::resize_target accordingly
No more placeholder color block — real rendered output visible
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes
userStories.notes: Added Arc<Device>/Arc<Queue> sharing to WgpuRenderDevice (from_arc_device ctor, device_arc/queue_arc accessors, render_world_offscreen, default_target_view, default_target_size). Modified CLI: RenderState uses Arc<Device>/Arc<Queue>, App stores WgpuRenderDevice. Added render_scene_view() that renders 3D world to offscreen texture and registers via update_egui_texture_from_wgpu_texture before each egui frame. ShellUiState gains ViewportTexture, editor_camera_yaw/pitch/distance/target. draw_render_viewport displays rendered texture as egui::Image via TextureId::User. viewport_panel handles right-click drag (orbit), middle-click drag (pan), scroll (zoom). build_editor_render_world+quat_look_at apply editor camera to extracted RenderWorld. Placeholder still available as fallback when no texture is registered. All tests pass, workspace compiles.

userStories.title: Integrate wgpu render texture into egui Game View
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the Game View panel to display the runtime camera's rendered output during Play Mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Game View panel in EditorShell displays rendered texture from game_view render target
During Play Mode, Game View renders from the runtime Scene's active Camera (not editor camera)
Game View aspect ratio matches panel dimensions, not the Scene View camera
When not in Play Mode, Game View shows last frame or 'Enter Play Mode to view' overlay
Resize of Game View panel triggers resize of game_view render target
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ProjectManifest loading from aster.project.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ProjectContext to load and parse aster.project.toml from a project directory.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectManifest struct: name, version, default_scene (PathBuf), asset_root (PathBuf, default 'assets'), build_config (PathBuf, default 'build.runtime-min.toml')
ProjectManifest::load(project_root: &Path) -> EngineResult<Self> reads and parses TOML
Missing file returns descriptive error (file path in message)
Invalid TOML returns parse error with line/column if available
Default values applied for missing optional fields
Unit test: loads examples/project/aster.project.toml successfully
cargo test -p engine-editor passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement AssetDatabase scanning from asset_root
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AssetDatabase::scan() to recursively walk asset_root and register files with correct ResourceKind based on extension.
acceptanceCriteria: AssetDatabase::scan(root: &Path) -> EngineResult<()> walks directory tree
Each file gets a ResourceMeta with: guid (UUID5 from relative path), path, kind, import_state (default: Pending)
Extension → ResourceKind mapping: png/jpg/jpeg→Texture, gltf/glb→Model, wav/ogg→Audio, wgsl→Shader, rhai→Script, json/toml→{check for scene/material/manifest format}
Directories create folder entries in the asset tree
Existing entries (matching path) are preserved, new files added, deleted files removed
Unit test: scan temporary directory with known file types, verify correct kinds
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Wire Hub Open action to construct ProjectContext and enter editor
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to select a project in Hub and click Open to enter the editor with my project loaded.
acceptanceCriteria: HubState::projects list populated from recent projects (stored in EditorPreferences)
Open button calls ProjectContext::open(project_path)
On success: EditorShell.screen = EditorScreen::Editor, shell holds Some(project_context)
On failure: ConsoleEntry pushed with error, stays on Hub screen
New Project flow: dialog collects name + location → creates directory skeleton → opens project
Project path added to recent projects list in preferences
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Bind Hierarchy panel to Scene GameObject tree
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the Hierarchy panel to show the actual scene object tree from the loaded Scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Hierarchy reads ProjectContext.scene.objects and displays one row per GameObject
Rows indented by parent-child depth, sorted by sibling_index
Each row shows object name + icon based on components (Camera=📷, Light=💡, Mesh=🔺, empty=📦 — or Unicode alternatives)
Empty scene shows localized 'No objects in scene' message
Changes to Scene.objects (add/remove/reparent) immediately reflected in Hierarchy
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Hierarchy interaction (select, rename, context menu)
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to click objects in Hierarchy to select them, double-click to rename, and right-click for context menu actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Single click: updates SelectionService to Selection::Entity(id), Inspector refreshes
Double-click slow: enters inline rename mode (text input), Enter confirms, Escape cancels
Right-click context menu: Delete, Duplicate, Create Empty Child, Create Camera, Create Light
Delete removes GameObject from Scene and records UndoCommand
Duplicate clones GameObject with new EntityId and appends ' (Copy)' to name
Selected row gets highlighted background using the palette selection color
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Bind Inspector to selected entity's ComponentData
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the Inspector panel to display all components of the selected entity from the Scene data.
acceptanceCriteria: Inspector reads SelectionService — when Selection::Entity(id), queries Scene for that GameObject
Header: object name (editable text field), Active checkbox, Tag/Layer labels
Each ComponentData entry rendered as a collapsible section with component type name as header
Component sections: Transform (always first), then Camera, MeshRenderer, Rigidbody, Collider, AudioSource, Light, Script in registration order
Empty selection shows 'Select an object to inspect' message
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Transform editing in Inspector
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to edit Position, Rotation, and Scale of a selected object with DragValue controls that update the Scene and Scene View in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Position: 3 DragValue (X, Y, Z) with step 0.1, range unbounded
Rotation: 3 DragValue for Euler angles (degrees), converted to Quat internally, step 1.0
Scale: 3 DragValue (X, Y, Z) with step 0.01, default 1.0 each
Changes update TransformHierarchy immediately and mark scene dirty
Changes recorded in UndoStack as single TransformChange command per drag session (not per frame)
Reset button next to each row resets to default (0/0/0 pos, 0/0/0 rot, 1/1/1 scale)
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ComponentData field editing for all component types
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to edit all component properties in the Inspector with appropriate controls per field type.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera: fov (DragValue 20-150), near_clip (DragValue 0.001-10), far_clip (DragValue 10-10000), clear_color (color picker or RGB DragValue), is_main (checkbox)
MeshRenderer: mesh_ref (text + browse button), material_ref (text + browse), cast_shadows (checkbox), receive_shadows (checkbox)
Rigidbody: body_type (dropdown: Static/Kinematic/Dynamic), mass (DragValue 0.01-10000), linear_damping, angular_damping, use_gravity (checkbox), lock_position/lock_rotation (axis checkboxes)
Collider: shape (dropdown: Box/Sphere/Capsule/Cylinder/Mesh), size/radius/height (DragValue), is_trigger (checkbox), physics_material (dropdown: default/metal/ice/rubber)
Light: kind (dropdown: Directional/Point/Spot), color (color picker), intensity (DragValue), range (DragValue for Point/Spot), spot_angle (DragValue for Spot)
AudioSource: audio_clip (text), volume (DragValue 0-1), play_on_start (checkbox), loop (checkbox), spatial_blend (DragValue 0-1)
Each change records in UndoStack
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Add Component dropdown and Remove Component
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to add new components to an entity via a searchable dropdown, and remove existing ones via a button.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Add Component' button at bottom of Inspector opens searchable dropdown
Dropdown lists all registered component types (Camera, MeshRenderer, Rigidbody, Collider, Light, AudioSource, Script)
Typing filters the list by component name (case insensitive)
Selecting a component adds new ComponentData with default values to the entity
Each component section header has 'Remove' button (X icon) — removes component after confirmation
Transform component does NOT show Remove button (required component)
Add/Remove recorded in UndoStack
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Scene save to JSON (Ctrl+S)
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to press Ctrl+S to save the current scene, with automatic backup of the previous file.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+S triggers Scene::to_json() → write to default_scene path
Before write: if file exists, copy to same path with .bak extension (overwrites previous .bak)
Write uses atomic strategy: write to .tmp file → fs::rename to target
Status bar shows 'Scene saved to <path>' briefly (3 seconds)
Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S) opens native file dialog, writes to chosen path, updates ProjectManifest.default_scene
Close project with unsaved changes shows 'Save changes before closing?' dialog (Save / Discard / Cancel)
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Scene load and Save As dialog
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to open a different scene file and use Save As to persist to a new path.
acceptanceCriteria: File > Open Scene (Ctrl+O) opens file dialog filtered to .json/.scene
Loading a new scene replaces current ProjectContext.scene, Hierarchy and Inspector refresh
If current scene has unsaved changes, prompt to save first
Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S) opens save dialog, writes to chosen path
After Save As, ProjectManifest.default_scene is NOT automatically updated (user must explicitly 'Set as Default Scene')
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Bind Project panel to AssetDatabase tree
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the Project panel to display the asset tree from AssetDatabase with type icons and import status.
acceptanceCriteria: Project panel reads ProjectContext.asset_db and displays folder tree
Each file row shows: icon (by ResourceKind), filename, import status badge (Pending=gray, Importing=yellow, Ready=green, Error=red)
Folders expand/collapse on click, default all expanded
Thumbnail column for Texture assets shows small preview (if imported)
Search bar at top filters files by name (case insensitive), hides non-matching entries
Empty project shows localized 'No assets in project' message
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Project panel interactions (select, double-click, drag-to-scene)
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want to interact with assets in the Project panel: select to see details, double-click to open, and drag onto the Scene View.
acceptanceCriteria: Single click: selects asset, shows metadata in info panel (path, GUID, size, import state, last modified)
Double-click: opens asset in appropriate way (scene→load, texture→preview popup, script→external editor, model→preview)
Right-click menu: Delete, Rename, Reimport, Show in File Manager, Copy GUID
Drag from Project panel to Scene View creates GameObject: model→MeshRenderer entity, texture→Sprite or material assignment, scene→additive load
Drag from Project panel to Inspector field (e.g., mesh_ref) assigns the asset reference
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RuntimeServices game loop frame ordering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RuntimeServices to execute a well-ordered game loop: input → fixed update → update → late update → render.
acceptanceCriteria: RuntimeServices::run_frame() executes in order: begin_frame (input reset) → fixed_timestep_loop → update → late_update → render_submit → deferred_destroy → end_frame
Game loop runs continuously via winit EventLoop::run() or EventLoop::pump_events()
Winit events (KeyboardInput, MouseInput, CursorMoved, MouseWheel, WindowResized, RedrawRequested) dispatched to RuntimeServices methods
WindowEvent::CloseRequested sets exit flag, loop terminates cleanly
Smoke test: run 60 frames in headless mode, assert frame_index == 60
cargo test -p runtime-min passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement fixed timestep accumulator with max_dt guard
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a proper fixed timestep accumulator to run physics and fixed update at a stable 60 Hz independent of frame rate.
acceptanceCriteria: TimeState holds accumulator: f32, incremented by delta_seconds each frame
While accumulator >= fixed_delta_seconds: execute fixed_update callbacks, subtract fixed_delta_seconds from accumulator
max_dt (default 0.1) caps delta_seconds to prevent spiral of death
After accumulator loop, remaining fraction (accumulator / fixed_dt) available for interpolation
Unit test: delta=0.05 with fixed_dt=1/60 should trigger 3 fixed updates, accumulator ~0.0
Unit test: delta=0.5 (capped to max_dt=0.1) triggers at most ceil(0.1/0.0167)=6 fixed updates
cargo test -p engine-core passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement winit event → InputState conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need winit window and input events converted to the engine's InputState each frame.
acceptanceCriteria: RuntimeServices::process_event(event: &winit::Event<()>) switches on event type
WindowEvent::KeyboardInput { virtual_keycode: Some(k), state } → InputState key Pressed/Released
WindowEvent::MouseInput { state, button } → mouse button Pressed/Released
WindowEvent::CursorMoved { position } → cursor_position updated (in logical pixels)
Mouse delta computed as (current_position - previous_position) each frame
WindowEvent::MouseWheel { delta } → scroll_delta accumulated
WindowEvent::Resized(size) → triggers swapchain resize + InputState window_size update
InputState::begin_frame() called at start of each iteration
cargo test -p runtime-min passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ActionMap integration with RuntimeServices
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want RuntimeServices to expose action-based input queries so script and component code doesn't hardcode keybindings.
acceptanceCriteria: RuntimeServices holds an ActionMap instance with default bindings loaded
RuntimeServices::action_pressed(name) delegates to ActionMap::action_pressed
RuntimeServices::action_held(name) delegates to ActionMap::action_held
ActionMap can be configured from project settings (action_bindings.toml in project root)
Default bindings: MoveForward, MoveBack, MoveLeft, MoveRight, Jump, Fire, Interact, Pause
Axes: MoveHorizontal (A/D or Left/Right → -1/0/1), MoveVertical (W/S or Up/Down → -1/0/1)
cargo test -p runtime-min passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Play Mode enter/exit with Scene deep copy
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want Play Mode to deep-copy the edit-time Scene, run the copy, and discard it on exit so edits are never lost.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene::enter_play_mode() serializes current scene to JSON, deserializes into play_scene, returns clone
Scene::exit_play_mode() discards play_scene, restores edit state from snapshot
RuntimeServices created with play_scene, not edit scene
On exit play mode, all RuntimeServices resources (physics world, scripts, render targets) cleaned up
EditorShell shows visual indicator when in Play Mode (tinted toolbar, orange border on Game View)
Unit test: modify scene in play mode, exit, verify original scene unchanged
cargo test -p engine-ecs passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Wire Toolbar Play/Pause/Stop to RuntimeServices control
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want the toolbar Play/Pause/Stop buttons to control Play Mode state.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar shows Play (▶), Pause (⏸), Stop (⏹) buttons
Play: calls enter_play_mode(), creates RuntimeServices, begins game loop, button toggles to Stop icon
Pause: toggles RuntimeServices.paused, button shows pressed state when paused
Stop: calls exit_play_mode(), destroys RuntimeServices, returns to edit mode
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+P = Play/Stop toggle, Ctrl+Shift+P = Pause toggle
Pause disabled (grayed out) when not in Play Mode
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RapierPhysicsBackend struct and body management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need engine-physics to provide a RapierPhysicsBackend that creates and destroys rigid bodies via Rapier.
acceptanceCriteria: RapierPhysicsBackend struct behind rapier feature flag on engine-physics
Fields: rigid_body_set (Rapier RigidBodySet), collider_set (ColliderSet), impulse_joint_set, integration_parameters, physics_pipeline, island_manager, broad_phase, narrow_phase, ccd_solver
PhysicsBackend::create_body(desc) creates Rapier RigidBody with type (Fixed/Dynamic/Kinematic), mass, damping, gravity setting → returns BodyHandle
PhysicsBackend::destroy_body(handle) removes body from rigid_body_set
Unit test: create dynamic body, step simulation, body falls due to gravity
Unit test: create static body, step simulation, body does not move
cargo test -p engine-physics --features rapier passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RapierPhysicsBackend collider management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RapierPhysicsBackend to create and attach colliders to bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysicsBackend::create_collider(desc) creates Rapier Collider from shape (Cuboid/Ball/Capsule/Cylinder), size, is_trigger, friction, restitution → returns ColliderHandle
PhysicsBackend::attach_collider(collider_handle, body_handle) attaches collider to body
PhysicsBackend::remove_collider(handle) removes collider from collider_set
Collider shapes supported: Box → Cuboid, Sphere → Ball, Capsule → Capsule, Cylinder → Cylinder
Layer membership set on collider creation
Unit test: create collider, attach to body, step, verify no crash
cargo test -p engine-physics --features rapier passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RapierPhysicsBackend step and spatial queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RapierPhysicsBackend to step the simulation and support raycast and overlap queries.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysicsBackend::step(dt) runs Rapier physics_pipeline.step() with gravity, integration_parameters
PhysicsBackend::raycast(origin, direction, max_distance) calls Rapier query_pipeline.cast_ray() → Option<RayHit>
RayHit contains: entity (body handle), point, normal, distance
PhysicsBackend::overlap_sphere(center, radius) calls query_pipeline.intersections_with_sphere() → Vec<OverlapResult>
OverlapResult contains: body_handle, collider_handle
Unit test: two spheres, raycast through them, verify closest hit returned
Unit test: overlap sphere query, verify intersecting bodies returned
cargo test -p engine-physics --features rapier passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement RapierPhysicsBackend contact and trigger events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RapierPhysicsBackend to collect collision and trigger events from the physics step.
acceptanceCriteria: RapierPhysicsBackend holds contact_event_buffer: Vec<ContactEvent>
After step(), drain Rapier ContactForceEvent and store as ContactEvent { body_a, body_b, collider_a, collider_b, entered (bool), contact_points }
Trigger events collected from Rapier IntersectionEvent when is_trigger colliders overlap
PhysicsBackend::drain_contacts() returns all buffered events and clears buffer
ContactEvents only generated for layer pairs allowed by LayerMatrix
LayerMatrix with default layers (0=Default, 1=Player, 2=Enemy, 3=Trigger, 4=Projectile)
Unit test: drop cube onto floor, verify contact event generated with entered=true
Unit test: move body through trigger zone, verify trigger enter + exit events
cargo test -p engine-physics --features rapier passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement PhysicsSync ECS-to-Physics creation and destruction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PhysicsSync to watch ECS for RigidbodyComponent/ColliderComponent changes and sync them to the PhysicsWorld.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysicsSync struct in engine-editor with reference to PhysicsWorld and ECS World
PhysicsSync::sync_creation() iterates scene entities: for each RigidbodyComponent with handle=None → create_body() → store handle
For each ColliderComponent with handle=None (and parent has RigidbodyComponent) → create_collider() → attach → store handle
PhysicsSync::sync_destruction() iterates destroyed entity IDs: remove colliders then remove body from PhysicsWorld
Sync called once per fixed update before physics step
Unit test: add RigidbodyComponent to entity → sync → body exists in PhysicsWorld
Unit test: destroy entity → sync → body removed from PhysicsWorld
cargo test -p engine-editor passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement PhysicsSync transform writeback after step
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PhysicsSync to read back RigidBody transforms after each physics step and update the ECS TransformHierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysicsSync::sync_transforms() after physics step: for each dynamic/kinematic body, read Rapier position and rotation
Write position/rotation to corresponding entity's TransformHierarchy
Static bodies skipped (no transform writeback needed)
Entities without RigidbodyComponent not affected (non-physics transforms preserved)
Writeback preserves local-vs-world transform distinction: updates world transform in hierarchy
Unit test: create dynamic body, step simulation, verify entity Transform position changed
cargo test -p engine-editor passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Create engine-script-rhai crate scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new engine-script-rhai crate with RhaiScriptBackend struct and basic engine initialization.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate engine-script-rhai added to workspace members
Cargo.toml with dependencies: rhai, engine-ecs, engine-core, engine-platform
Feature flag script-rhai on engine-script-rhai
RhaiScriptBackend struct: holds rhai::Scope, rhai::Engine, AST cache (HashMap<PathBuf, rhai::AST>)
RhaiScriptBackend::new() -> Self initializes Rhai engine with default modules disabled (only core)
Added to workspace Cargo.toml as workspace dependency
cargo build -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement Rhai script loading and compilation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RhaiScriptBackend to load .rhai files from the asset root, compile them to AST, and cache them.
acceptanceCriteria: RhaiScriptBackend::load_script(path: &Path) reads .rhai file, compiles to AST, stores in cache keyed by path
Compilation errors returned as EngineError with file path and line number
RhaiScriptBackend::load_scene_scripts(scene: &Scene) finds all ScriptComponentProxy with backend='rhai', loads each script path
Script path resolution: relative to asset_root (project:/) or builtin:/ prefix
AST cache reused across entities using the same script file
Unit test: load valid .rhai file → Ok(AST)
Unit test: load .rhai with syntax error → Err with line number
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ScriptContext lifecycle hooks
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want Rhai scripts to receive on_start, on_update(dt), and on_fixed_update(fixed_dt) callbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: RhaiScriptBackend::run_start(entity_id, script_path) calls on_start() function if defined in script
RhaiScriptBackend::run_update(entity_id, script_path, dt) calls on_update(dt) if defined
RhaiScriptBackend::run_fixed_update(entity_id, script_path, fixed_dt) calls on_fixed_update(fixed_dt) if defined
Scripts receive entity_id as global constant 'self_entity' accessible in script
Missing lifecycle function in script is NOT an error — silently skip
Script runs with its own scope (persistent across calls for same entity)
Unit test: script defines on_start → call run_start → verify side effect (set variable in scope)
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ScriptContext input API
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want Rhai scripts to query input state: is_pressed, axis values, and mouse delta.
acceptanceCriteria: Rhai module 'input' injected into script scope before each lifecycle call
input::is_pressed(key_name: &str) -> bool reads from RuntimeServices.input_state
input::is_held(key_name: &str) -> bool
input::is_released(key_name: &str) -> bool
input::axis(axis_name: &str) -> f32 (MoveHorizontal: -1/0/1, MoveVertical: -1/0/1)
input::mouse_delta() -> (f32, f32) returns x/y delta since last frame
input::mouse_position() -> (f32, f32) returns current cursor position
Unit test: set input state, run script that queries is_pressed('W'), verify returns true
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ScriptContext transform API
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want Rhai scripts to read and write the entity's transform.
acceptanceCriteria: Rhai module 'transform' injected into script scope for the owning entity
transform::get_position() -> (f32, f32, f32)
transform::set_position(x, y, z)
transform::get_rotation() -> (f32, f32, f32, f32) as quaternion (x, y, z, w)
transform::set_rotation(x, y, z, w)
transform::get_scale() -> (f32, f32, f32)
transform::set_scale(x, y, z)
transform::translate(dx, dy, dz) — adds to current position
transform::look_at(tx, ty, tz) — sets rotation to face target point
Unit test: script sets position via transform API, verify TransformHierarchy updated
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ScriptContext spawn/destroy and physics query API
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want Rhai scripts to spawn and destroy entities, and query the physics world.
acceptanceCriteria: Rhai module 'world' injected: spawn(name: &str) -> String (entity_id), destroy(id: String)
spawn creates entity with empty GameObject, returns EntityId as string, deferred to end of frame
destroy marks entity for deferred destruction at end of frame
Rhai module 'physics' injected: raycast(ox, oy, oz, dx, dy, dz, max_dist) -> f32 or () if no hit
physics::overlap_sphere(cx, cy, cz, radius) -> Array of entity_id strings
get_resource(path: &str) -> String (GUID) resolves project:/ or builtin:/ paths through AssetDatabase
Unit test: spawn entity via script, verify it exists in scene deferred queue
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement script error handling and ConsoleEntry reporting
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want script errors to appear in the Console with file path and line number, without crashing the engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Rhai runtime errors caught by RhaiScriptBackend and converted to ConsoleEntry
ConsoleEntry: level=Error, source.subsystem='script', source.file=script_path, source.line=rhai_error.line(), message=error.to_string()
Script with compilation errors sets ScriptComponentProxy.pending_recovery=true
pending_recovery scripts skipped during lifecycle calls (no crash, no error spam each frame)
Script runtime errors do NOT set pending_recovery (transient errors, e.g., divide by zero, still call next frame)
Script panic (via Rhai panic()) caught and converted to ConsoleEntry with level=Error
Unit test: load script with syntax error → ConsoleEntry emitted, pending_recovery set
Unit test: script with runtime panic → ConsoleEntry emitted, script still called next frame
cargo test -p engine-script-rhai passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement PngImporter (read, decode, mip chain, CpuTextureResource)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need engine-assets to import PNG files into CpuTextureResource with mip chains for GPU upload.
acceptanceCriteria: PngImporter::import(path: &Path, options: &ImportOptions) -> EngineResult<ImportOutcome>
Reads file, decodes PNG via image crate, extracts RGBA8 pixel data
Generates mip chain: each level half resolution of previous, box-filter downsampling, down to 1x1
All mip levels stored in CpuTextureResource { width, height, format: Rgba8UnormSrgb, mip_levels: Vec<Vec<u8>> }
ImportOutcome with resource: CpuResource::Texture, diagnostics: empty on success
Corrupted PNG → ImportOutcome with diagnostics containing error message, no panic
Unit test: import valid PNG fixture → Ok with texture resource and empty diagnostics
Unit test: import invalid file → Ok with at least one diagnostic
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement GltfImporter mesh extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need engine-assets to extract mesh geometry (positions, normals, uvs, indices) from glTF files.
acceptanceCriteria: GltfImporter::import(path: &Path) -> EngineResult<ImportOutcome>
Parses glTF JSON + binary buffer via gltf crate
For each mesh primitive: extracts POSITION, NORMAL, TEXCOORD_0 accessors into Vec<Vertex>
Extracts indices from INDEX accessor into Vec<u32>
Produces CpuMeshResource per mesh: { name, vertices, indices, bounds (AABB) }
Multiple meshes in glTF → Vec<CpuMeshResource>
Unit test: import valid glTF fixture → mesh resource with expected vertex/index counts
Unit test: glTF without normals → vertex normals filled as (0,1,0) default
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement GltfImporter material and texture extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need engine-assets to extract material parameters and texture references from glTF files.
acceptanceCriteria: For each glTF material: extract pbrMetallicRoughness { baseColorFactor, metallicFactor, roughnessFactor }
Extract baseColorTexture reference (index + texCoord), normalTexture, metallicRoughnessTexture
Extract emissiveFactor, alphaMode (OPAQUE/BLEND/MASK), alphaCutoff
Produce CpuMaterialResource per material: { name, base_color, metallic, roughness, base_color_texture_ref, normal_texture_ref }
Texture references stored as relative asset paths to be resolved by AssetDatabase
Unit test: glTF with PBR material → material resource with correct metallic/roughness values
Unit test: glTF without materials → default material (white, metallic=0, roughness=0.5)
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement ImportQueue worker thread with GPU upload callback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ImportQueue to process imports on a background thread and schedule GPU uploads on the render thread.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportQueue::spawn_worker() starts std::thread for CPU import processing
Worker receives ImportJob via crossbeam or std::sync::mpsc channel
ImportJob { asset_path, resource_kind, import_options } → dispatched to correct importer by extension
ImportOutcome sent back via callback or stored in Atomic/Arc<Mutex<VecDeque>>
GPU upload queue: completed CPU imports stored in VecDeque<GpuUploadTask>
GpuUploadTask consumed each frame on render thread via ImportQueue::drain_gpu_uploads()
Integration test: enqueue PNG import → worker processes → GpuUploadTask available
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement FileWatcher for asset change detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a file watcher on asset_root that detects file changes and marks affected assets as stale.
acceptanceCriteria: FileWatcher::start(asset_root: &Path) spawns notify watcher thread
FileWatcher::poll_events() returns Vec<FileEvent> { path, kind: Created|Modified|Removed|Renamed }
Modified events debounced: within 200ms window, only latest event per path emitted
EditorShell calls poll_events each frame, routes events to AssetDatabase::handle_event
File modified → asset marked Stale, re-import queued
File created → new ResourceMeta with Pending state added to AssetDatabase
File removed → ResourceMeta removed from AssetDatabase + loaded GPU resources enqueued for deferred destroy
Integration test: write file to watched dir, verify event emitted within 500ms
cargo test -p engine-assets passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement hot reload flow (stale mark → reimport → GPU swap)
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want modified assets to automatically reimport and swap in without restarting the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: AssetDatabase::handle_event(Modified(path)): lookup asset by path, set import_state=Stale, enqueue reimport
Reimport produces new CpuResource, queued for GPU upload
GPU upload callback: new GPU resource replaces old in GPU resource registry, old resource enqueued for deferred destroy (N frames delay, default 3)
Referencing entities (MeshRenderer, Material) automatically use new GPU resource on next render
Reimport errors → asset import_state=Error, old resource kept, error logged to Console
Integration test: modify PNG file on disk, verify Scene View updates texture within 3 seconds
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI run subcommand
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want `cargo run -p engine-cli -- run examples/project` to open a window and run the game.
acceptanceCriteria: engine-cli `run <project_path>` subcommand using clap
Loads ProjectManifest → Scene → AssetDatabase from project_path
Creates winit Window with title from ProjectManifest.name
Constructs RuntimeServices with WgpuRenderDevice, RapierPhysicsBackend, RhaiScriptBackend
Runs game loop via winit EventLoop::run()
Escape key or close button exits cleanly (no panic on drop)
Smoke test: run examples/project, verify process exits with code 0 after window close
cargo build -p engine-cli passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI build subcommand with BuildPipeline
userStories.description: As a game developer, I want `cargo run -p engine-cli -- build examples/project` to produce a self-contained output directory.
acceptanceCriteria: engine-cli `build <project_path>` subcommand using clap
Reads BuildConfiguration from build.runtime-min.toml (or --config path)
Validates BuildConfiguration via diagnostics(), exits with error if invalid
Invokes cargo build (with --release if config.release=true, --target if set)
On success: copies runtime binary to <output>/bin/, generates assets_manifest.json, copies default scene to <output>/scenes/, copies import_cache.json if exists
Writes build_info.json: { timestamp, target, release, engine_version }
On cargo build failure: prints stderr, exits non-zero
Integration test: build examples/project, verify output directory structure
cargo build -p engine-cli passes
cargo check --workspace --all-features passes

userStories.title: Add windowless scene simulation test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an automated test that runs the full game loop headlessly with physics and verifies entities move.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in runtime-min/tests/: creates RuntimeServices with headless renderer + Rapier physics
Scene with one GameObject carrying RigidbodyComponent (dynamic) + ColliderComponent (box, on ground plane)
Run 60 fixed-timestep ticks (1 second simulated)
Assert entity's transform position.y changed (gravity pulled it down) or collided with ground
Assert no NaN or infinity in transform components
Existing smoke_runtime_min test still passes (frame_index == 1)
cargo test -p runtime-min --features runtime-game passes

userStories.title: Add Scene serialization golden round-trip tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need golden tests verifying Scene JSON round-trip integrity for all component types.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in engine-ecs/tests/: create Scene with 1 Camera, 1 MeshRenderer, 1 Rigidbody, 1 Collider, 1 AudioSource
Serialize to JSON → deserialize → assert equal to original
Serialize deserialized scene again → assert byte-identical JSON to first serialization
Test with empty scene → round-trip
Test with nested GameObject hierarchy → parent-child relationships preserved
cargo test -p engine-ecs passes

userStories.title: Add importer fixture tests (PNG, glTF valid and invalid)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated tests verifying importers handle both valid and invalid input correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test fixtures stored in engine-assets/tests/fixtures/: valid.png, valid.gltf, invalid.png (corrupted), invalid.gltf (malformed JSON)
PNG valid fixture → import succeeds, texture resource non-empty, diagnostics empty
PNG invalid fixture → import succeeds (no panic), at least one AssetDiagnostic
glTF valid fixture → import succeeds, mesh resource has expected vertex count, diagnostics empty
glTF invalid fixture → import succeeds (no panic), at least one AssetDiagnostic

userStories.title: Add editor state tests (open project, save, play mode)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests verifying editor core workflows: open project, save scene, enter/exit play mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in engine-editor-ui/tests/: construct EditorShell, call open project with examples/project path
Assert Hierarchy contains entries for objects defined in default_scene.json
Test: open project → modify GameObject name → save to temp path → reload → name preserved
Test: open project → enter Play Mode → assert ShellUiState.playing == true → exit → edit scene unchanged
Tests use headless renderer (no GPU needed)
